channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Scammers hit Christmas cheer with fake wine and festive gift emails - here's how to stay safe
item.description: Holiday-themed emails increasingly carry scams using loan forms, fake order confirmations, and credential harvesting to collect sensitive personal and financial information.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Inoue vs Picasso: live stream boxing online, start time, ringwalks, fight card, PPV cost
item.description: All the ways to watch Inoue vs Picasso live streams online, including PPV options as the Monster defends his undisputed super bantamweight crown against El Rey.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: Apple's iPad in 2025: the highs, lows, and what we want to see in 2026
item.description: Apple’s 2025 iPad lineup wasn’t about flashy redesigns, but meaningful performance upgrades and the arrival of iPadOS 26 combined to deliver the most complete iPad experience yet.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Manchester United vs Newcastle live streams: How to watch Premier League 2025-26 from anywhere in the world, team news
item.description: All the ways to watch Manchester United vs Newcastle live streams online and from anywhere in the only Premier League fixture on Boxing Day.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Back, TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: How we can live with AI without losing our humanity
item.description: The Fox News AI Newsletter covers the latest artificial intelligence technology advancements, including the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoD expands login options beyond CAC
item.description: The memo does not include any requirements for authenticators to be phishing-resistant, even though DoD is required to retire phishable authenticators by 2027. The post DoD expands login options beyond CAC first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: After LLMs and agents, the next AI frontier: video language models
item.description: Tesla’s viral videos show its Optimus humanoid robot serving drinks to guests — a glimpse of AI in the real world that a new AI innovation, called world models, is expected to make more reliable. (For one, humanoid robots will do a better job in navigating and serving people their custom drinks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Acemagic just launched one of the weirdest-looking Ryzen AI Max+ 395 mini PCs to date - Tank M1A Pro+ supports 24TB storage, quad 8K screens and claims to match the RTX 4070M laptop GPU
item.description: Acemagic’s Tank M1A Pro+ joins the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 mini PC wave with support for four 8K screens, 128GB memory and up to 24TB SSD storage.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Australia vs England: live stream Boxing Day 4th Ashes Test for *FREE* from anywhere in the world, Australia lead by 46 runs after 20 wickets fall on Day 1
item.description: All the ways to watch the 4th Ashes Test at the MCG, the famous Boxing Day Test, from anywhere as Australia and England battle in cricket’s historic series.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone scam tricks owners into giving phones away
item.description: New iPhone replacement scam uses pressure tactics and fake carrier calls to steal devices from buyers. Criminals claim shipping errors and demand urgent returns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 surprising ways to use AI
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. I like pushing AI to be less predictable. When AI assistants are less bland and more bold, they challenge my blind spots and nudge me to rethink.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon adds controversial AI facial recognition to Ring
item.description: Amazon Ring's new facial recognition feature sparks privacy controversy as Electronic Frontier Foundation critics argue the AI upgrade expands surveillance risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Morning Show season 5: everything we know about the Apple TV show's return
item.description: The Morning Show will return for a season five – here's everything we know about the return of Apple TV's most-watched shows.
item.dc:creator: Laura Martin, TechRadar

item.title: The DXC program empowering neurodivergent IT pros
item.description: The tech industry stands to gain significant value by tapping into neurodivergent talent. Such professionals can offer fresh perspectives, demonstrate strengths in areas critical to IT, and even have the potential to make teams 30% more productive, according to research from Deloitte.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 4 mandates for CIOs to bridge the AI trust gap
item.description: For the third consecutive year, the Thinkers360 AI Trust Index has taken the pulse of sentiment toward AI, and the results, once again, are a stark reminder to CIOs and CXOs that the technological innovation curve continues to outpace the ethical and governance structure required to support it.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to watch Royal Christmas Message 2025
item.description: King Charles III will deliver the traditional festive broadcast for the fourth time. Here's how to watch Royal Christmas Message 2025.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: Virtual reality opens doors for older people to build closer connections in real life
item.description: Many retirement developments serve as a tranquil refuge for older people who can no longer travel to faraway places or engage in bold adventures.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why TikTok’s ‘For You’ page is so valuable and what’s to change for U.S. users
item.description: Chinese tech giant ByteDance has signed an agreement to sell a majority stake in its video platform TikTok to a group of U.S. investors. President Donald Trump announced a preliminary agreement for the sale on Sept. 19, 2025, following his negotiation with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Is the Internet's time wrong? NIST warns servers may have lapsed due to a power outage
item.description: NIST warns specific public time servers may be inaccurate after a power outage disrupted atomic clock distribution.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch Midnight Mass 2025
item.description: The BBC bring live coverage of the First Mass of the Nativity. Here's how to watch Midnight Mass 2025 online and from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wardley, TechRadar

item.title: Trump’s $100,000 H-1B visa application fee upheld by judge
item.description: A federal judge said the Trump administration can move ahead with a $100,000 fee on new H-1B visa applications, providing a setback for US technology companies that rely on hiring skilled foreign workers.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Pro-Russian hackers claim cyberattack on French postal service
item.description: French prosecutors say a pro-Russian hacking group has claimed responsibility for a major cyberattack on France’s national postal service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI power tools: 6 ways to supercharge your terminal
item.description: The command line has always been the bedrock of the developer’s world. Since time immemorial, the CLI was a static place defined by the REPL (read-evaluate-print-loop). But now modern AI tools are changing that.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get started with Python’s new native JIT
item.description: JITing, or “just-in-time” compilation, can make relatively slow interpreted languages much faster. Until recently, JITting was available for Python only in the form of specialized third-party libraries, like Numba, or alternate versions of the Python interpreter, like PyPy.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bolivia lifts restrictions on satellite companies like Starlink to upgrade its internet connectivity
item.description: Bolivia’s new government has issued a decree that will allow global satellite internet companies such as Starlink or Kuiper to provide internet access across the Andean nation as it tries to upgrade its technology and speed up its notoriously slow connectivity rates.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Valdez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rust vision group seeks enumeration of language design goals
item.description: To help the Rust language continue scaling across domains and usage levels, the Rust Vision Doc group recommends enumerating the design goals for evolving the language while also improving the crates package system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google’s old assistant stays on Android for a while longer
item.description: In March, Computer Sweden reported that Google Assistant would be replaced by the AI tool Gemini in Android-based mobile phones before the new year. With just over a week to go until the end of the year, it is clear that this will not be the case.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data center investments break all records
item.description: New data centers are being built around the world in connection with AI initiatives. According to a compilation by S&P Global, spending will reach a record $61 billion in 2025, with the US and Canada accounting for the vast majority.
item.dc:creator: Network World
